items.requirement: Chrome DevTools browser workflow passes 23/23 and saves a durable browser UX report.
items.evidence: Browser UX verification records 23 passing workflow checks, zero failures, screenshots, and blocked traversal evidence.

items.requirement: Sample-case selection works in the normal reviewer path without manually clearing default text.
items.evidence: Browser-style sample selection now records sample_case mode and renders the selected synthetic sample.

items.requirement: Landing input UX makes sample, pasted text, and uploaded file precedence clear.
items.evidence: Landing copy explains sample, pasted text, and upload precedence.

items.requirement: Receipt JSON and Markdown links work in the live local browser and artifact serving blocks traversal.
items.evidence: Receipt JSON/Markdown links use a scoped artifact route and traversal probes return non-OK responses.

items.requirement: Result-page Deeper Dive remains navigable and summary-first.
items.evidence: Deeper Dive navigation and summary-first result checks remain green in rendered and browser verification.

items.requirement: Node audit OK, Adjust, and Re-check behavior remains functional and traceable.
items.evidence: OK, Adjust, and Re-check workflow behavior remains traceable and graph authority stays separate.

items.requirement: Safety and provenance boundaries remain intact.
items.evidence: Local deterministic governance-review boundaries and comparison-only model boundaries remain documented.

items.requirement: Regression tests cover the two browser-discovered failures.
items.evidence: Regression tests cover sample precedence, live receipt links, traversal blocking, and durable browser report shape.

items.requirement: Documentation and status artifacts describe the remediated browser behavior.
items.evidence: Docs and progress artifacts describe corrected sample input semantics, artifact links, browser verification, and readiness impact.
Final verification report records passing pytest, validation commands, UX verification, JSON syntax, pip dry-run, and git diff checks.
